questions.q: Which great epic is the sage Valmiki traditionally credited with composing?
questions.explanation: Valmiki is revered as the author of the Ramayana, the epic narrating the life of Rama.

questions.q: By what honorific title, meaning 'first poet,' is the sage Valmiki traditionally known?
questions.explanation: Valmiki is honoured as the adi-kavi, meaning the 'first poet' of the Sanskrit tradition.

questions.q: Who is the central hero whose life is narrated in Valmiki's Ramayana?
questions.explanation: The Ramayana composed by Valmiki narrates the life and deeds of Rama, prince of Ayodhya.

questions.q: In which language did the sage Valmiki compose the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Valmiki composed the Ramayana in Sanskrit, in the classical shloka verse form.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, the killing of which bird by a hunter is said to have moved Valmiki to utter his first verse?
questions.explanation: Valmiki's grief at a hunter slaying a mating krauncha (crane) is said to have spontaneously formed the first shloka.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, where does the sage Valmiki give shelter to the exiled Sita?
questions.explanation: In the Uttara Kanda, Valmiki shelters the banished Sita in his forest hermitage.

questions.q: What are the names of Sita's twin sons, whom Valmiki raises and teaches in his hermitage?
questions.explanation: Valmiki nurtures and instructs Sita's twin sons, Lava and Kusha, in his hermitage.

questions.q: According to tradition, what did Valmiki teach Lava and Kusha to recite in his hermitage?
questions.explanation: Valmiki taught his own composition, the Ramayana, to Sita's sons Lava and Kusha.

questions.q: What kind of figure is the sage Valmiki traditionally described as in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Valmiki is portrayed as a venerated rishi (sage) and poet who composed the Ramayana.

questions.q: The emotion of grief that arose in Valmiki upon the crane's death is described in the tradition as giving rise to which poetic mood?
questions.explanation: Valmiki's sorrow (shoka) is said to have transformed into shloka, expressing the karuna (pathos) rasa.

questions.q: In Valmiki's Ramayana, which deity is traditionally said to have inspired or commanded the sage to compose the epic?
questions.explanation: According to the Ramayana's opening, the god Brahma appears to Valmiki and bids him compose the story of Rama.

questions.q: From which celestial sage does Valmiki first hear the summary of Rama's story at the start of the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: In the Bala Kanda, the divine sage Narada relates the essence of Rama's story to Valmiki.

questions.q: The metrical verse form said to have originated from Valmiki's lament is known by which name?
questions.explanation: Valmiki's spontaneous lament is traditionally said to have taken the form of the anushtubh shloka metre.

questions.q: Approximately how many verses (shlokas) is Valmiki's Ramayana traditionally said to contain?
questions.explanation: Valmiki's Ramayana is traditionally counted at about twenty-four thousand shlokas.

questions.q: Into how many books or 'kandas' is Valmiki's Ramayana traditionally divided?
questions.explanation: Valmiki's Ramayana is conventionally arranged into seven kandas (books).

questions.q: Which kanda of Valmiki's Ramayana, dealing with later events, contains the episode of Sita's stay in his hermitage?
questions.explanation: The Uttara Kanda relates the later episodes, including Sita finding refuge in Valmiki's hermitage.

questions.q: The Sanskrit word 'shoka', central to the legend of Valmiki's first verse, means what?
questions.explanation: In the tradition, Valmiki's shoka (grief) at the crane's death gave rise to the shloka, the first verse.

questions.q: In Valmiki's Ramayana, on which occasion do Lava and Kusha publicly recite the epic before Rama?
questions.explanation: In the Uttara Kanda, Lava and Kusha sing the Ramayana before Rama during his Ashvamedha sacrifice.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, Valmiki is said to vouch for the purity of which character before the assembled court?
questions.explanation: In the Uttara Kanda, the sage Valmiki affirms Sita's purity before Rama and the court.

questions.q: The very first major poetic work of Sanskrit literature, attributed to Valmiki, gives him a reputation as the originator of which literary form?
questions.explanation: Valmiki's Ramayana is regarded as the first kavya, earning him the title adi-kavi, founder of refined poetry.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, before the crane episode, where does the sage Valmiki go that leads to his witnessing the hunter's act?
options: To the summit of Mount Meru
To the banks of the Tamasa river
questions.explanation: Valmiki had gone to bathe at the Tamasa river when he witnessed the slaying of the crane.

questions.q: In Valmiki's Ramayana, who carries Sita away to the hermitage where Valmiki receives her?
questions.explanation: In the Uttara Kanda, Lakshmana escorts the exiled Sita to the forest near Valmiki's hermitage.

questions.q: Which word in Valmiki's curse upon the hunter became, by tradition, the model for the epic's whole verse structure?
questions.explanation: Valmiki's verse beginning 'ma nishada' (let you find no rest, O hunter) is traditionally cited as the first shloka.

questions.q: According to later tradition, by what earlier name or vocation is Valmiki said to have been known before becoming a sage?
options: A hunter or highwayman named Ratnakara
questions.explanation: Later traditions relate that Valmiki, once known as Ratnakara, lived as a hunter or robber before his transformation into a sage.

questions.q: In the popular legend of his transformation, repeating which sacred name is said to have turned Valmiki from his former life toward asceticism?
questions.explanation: Legend holds that Valmiki attained sagehood by ceaselessly repeating the name 'Rama' during long meditation.

questions.q: The name 'Valmiki' is popularly explained from 'valmika', a Sanskrit word meaning what, which is said to have encased him during meditation?
questions.explanation: The name Valmiki is traditionally derived from valmika (anthill), said to have grown over him as he meditated.

questions.q: Which kanda of Valmiki's Ramayana opens the epic and contains the legend of the first verse and Brahma's command?
questions.explanation: The Bala Kanda, the first book, narrates the origin of the shloka and Brahma's bidding to Valmiki.

questions.q: In Valmiki's Ramayana, what does Sita do at the end of the Uttara Kanda after her sons are reconciled with Rama?
options: She is received back into the earth by her mother Bhumi
She returns to rule beside Rama for many years
She becomes an ascetic in Valmiki's hermitage forever
She journeys to Lanka to free other captives
questions.explanation: In the Uttara Kanda, Sita calls upon the earth-goddess Bhumi, who opens to receive her daughter.

questions.q: Valmiki's Ramayana is most often classified within Hindu literature as which kind of text, distinct from the Vedas and Puranas?
questions.explanation: Valmiki's Ramayana is classed as an Itihasa, an epic history, alongside the Mahabharata.

questions.q: Within Valmiki's Ramayana, who is described as the divine source from whom Rama is an incarnation, the avatar whose deeds the epic celebrates?
questions.explanation: Valmiki's Ramayana presents Rama as an avatar of Vishnu, born to vanquish the demon Ravana.
